agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier context. A user (shipper/sender) wants to cancel a previously placed delivery order. Cancellation eligibility and consequences depend heavily on where the order sits in its lifecycle — an order that has already been picked up by a courier is fundamentally different from one that is still awaiting assignment.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for courier pickup and delivery of a package from an origin to a destination, with associated sender, recipient, pricing, and SLA details."},{"term":"Order Status","definition":"The current lifecycle state of an order (e.g., Pending, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled)."},{"term":"Cancellation","definition":"A user-initiated action that terminates an active order before successful delivery, triggering refund and operational reversal workflows."},{"term":"Cancellation Window","definition":"The period during which an order is eligible for cancellation — determined by its current status in the fulfillment lifecycle."},{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver/rider assigned to fulfill the delivery order."},{"term":"Refund","definition":"The return of payment (full or partial) to the user upon cancellation, the amount determined by the cancellation policy tier."},{"term":"Restocking / Return-to-Sender","definition":"The physical reversal of a picked-up package back to its origin when cancellation occurs after courier possession."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"An order may only be cancelled if its status is one of: Pending, Assigned, or PickedUp. Orders in status Delivered, Already Cancelled, or Failed are not cancellable.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing cancellation of a delivered or already-cancelled order would corrupt order state and financial records. A delivered package cannot be un-delivered through cancellation — that is a separate Returns process.","testable_assertion":"Given an order with status Delivered, when the user requests cancellation, then the system must reject the request with a clear error."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A cancelled order's status must transition to Cancelled and must never transition to any subsequent fulfillment state (Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Cancelled is a terminal state. Allowing a cancelled order to resume fulfillment would violate state machine integrity and could result in unauthorized deliveries and duplicate charges.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Cancelled, when any state transition is attempted, then the system must reject it."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Cancellation before courier assignment (status: Pending) entitles the user to a full refund.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund tiers are a business policy. A VP could change the refund percentage at any time without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Pending, when the user cancels, then the refund amount equals 100% of the charged amount."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Cancellation after courier assignment but before pickup (status: Assigned) entitles the user to a partial refund minus a cancellation fee, because the courier has already been dispatched.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The cancellation fee amount is a configurable business policy, not a system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Assigned, when the user cancels, then the refund amount equals the charged amount minus the configured cancellation fee."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Cancellation after pickup (status: PickedUp/InTransit) requires a return-to-sender workflow to be initiated and the user receives a partial refund minus cancellation and return shipping fees.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Post-pickup cancellation policy and associated fees are business decisions. The requirement to initiate return-to-sender is operational policy.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status PickedUp, when the user cancels, then a return-to-sender task is created for the assigned courier AND the refund equals the charged amount minus cancellation and return fees."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Only the order creator (sender) may cancel the order. Recipients and other users must not be able to cancel.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing arbitrary users to cancel orders they did not create is a security and data integrity violation. The sender owns the contractual relationship.","testable_assertion":"Given a user who is not the order creator, when they attempt to cancel the order, then the system must reject the request with an authorization error."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"When an order is cancelled that has an assigned courier, the courier must be notified of the cancellation and unassigned from the order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Failing to notify and unassign the courier results in wasted courier time, incorrect courier availability state, and potential unauthorized delivery attempts. This is operational integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given an order in status Assigned with a courier, when the order is cancelled, then the courier receives a cancellation notification AND the courier is unassigned from the order."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"A cancellation event must be recorded with timestamp, initiating user, order status at time of cancellation, and refund amount for audit purposes.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Financial and operational audit trail integrity. Without this record, disputes cannot be resolved and financial reconciliation breaks.","testable_assertion":"Given any successful cancellation, then a CancellationEvent is persisted containing: orderId, userId, previousStatus, cancellationTimestamp, and refundAmount."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An order in status Pending","when":"The order creator requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a full refund is initiated, and a CancellationEvent is recorded."},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An order in status Assigned with a courier","when":"The order creator requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, the courier is notified and unassigned, a partial refund (minus cancellation fee) is initiated, and a CancellationEvent is recorded."},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An order in status PickedUp (courier has the package)","when":"The order creator requests cancellation","then":"The order status transitions to Cancelled, a return-to-sender task is created for the courier, the courier is notified, a partial refund (minus cancellation + return fees) is initiated, and a CancellationEvent is recorded."},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An order in status Delivered","when":"The order creator requests cancellation","then":"The system rejects the cancellation with a clear message that delivered orders cannot be cancelled and directs the user to the returns process."},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An order in status Cancelled","when":"The order creator requests cancellation again","then":"The system rejects the request with a message that the order is already cancelled. No duplicate refund is issued."},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A user who is NOT the order creator","when":"They attempt to cancel the order","then":"The system returns an authorization error and the order remains unchanged."},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An order in status Assigned and the cancellation is processed","when":"The courier checks their active assignments","then":"The cancelled order no longer appears in their assignment list."},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A successful cancellation at any eligible status","when":"An admin queries the audit log","then":"A CancellationEvent exists with orderId, userId, previousStatus, timestamp, and refundAmount."},{"id":"AC-9","given":"An order in status Pending","when":"The order creator cancels and then the system attempts to assign a courier","then":"The assignment is rejected because the order is in terminal state Cancelled."},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A cancellation request is submitted but the refund service is unavailable","when":"The refund initiation fails","then":"The cancellation must NOT be committed. The order remains in its original status and the user is informed to retry. No partial state change occurs."}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Race condition: courier picks up the package at the exact moment the user submits cancellation","expected_behavior":"The system must resolve this atomically. Either the cancellation succeeds (and triggers return-to-sender at the PickedUp-tier refund) or the pickup wins and the user is informed the order has progressed past the cancellable window they initiated from. The order must never end up in an inconsistent state where it is both Cancelled and actively in transit without a return task."},{"scenario":"Double-cancel: user rapidly clicks cancel twice","expected_behavior":"Only one cancellation is processed. The second request is rejected as already cancelled. Only one refund is issued. Idempotency must be enforced."},{"scenario":"Cancellation during payment processing (order not yet fully confirmed)","expected_behavior":"If payment has not settled, the cancellation should void the pending charge rather than issuing a refund. The order transitions to Cancelled."},{"scenario":"Courier is unreachable when cancellation notification is sent","expected_behavior":"The system must retry courier notification with backoff. The cancellation itself is still committed (the order is Cancelled), but the courier notification is tracked as pending and retried. Dispatch/operations is alerted if notification fails after max retries."},{"scenario":"Partial multi-package order cancellation","expected_behavior":"If the domain supports multi-package orders, the spec must clarify whether partial cancellation (cancel one package but not others) is allowed. If not explicitly supported, cancellation applies to the entire order atomically."},{"scenario":"Cancellation of an order with active promotional discount or voucher","expected_behavior":"The refund amount must account for the actual amount charged (post-discount), not the list price. Any single-use voucher/promo code should be restored to the user's account for reuse."},{"scenario":"Cancellation requested by a user whose account is suspended or flagged for fraud","expected_behavior":"The cancellation may be allowed (the user is still the order creator), but the refund should be held for manual review rather than auto-processed. Flag for the fraud/trust team."},{"scenario":"Order created via API integration (B2B partner) — who can cancel?","expected_behavior":"The cancellation authorization rule (BR-6) must extend to API keys/service accounts. The originating integration account is treated as the order creator. Partner portal users must be authorized against the partner's order, not just any order."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Cancellation fees offset operational costs of wasted courier dispatch. Incorrect refund logic (e.g., full refund after pickup) directly erodes margin. Double-refund bugs are a direct financial loss. Proper cancellation-tier refund policy balances customer satisfaction against operational cost recovery.","operational":"Courier unassignment and notification are critical — failure to unassign creates phantom assignments that degrade courier utilization metrics and route optimization. Return-to-sender workflows after pickup add operational load and must be tracked. Audit trail is essential for dispute resolution with both customers and couriers.","user":"Users expect a clear, fast cancellation experience with transparent refund communication. Ambiguous cancellability (unclear what states allow cancellation) erodes trust. Users must understand WHY a cancellation was rejected and WHAT their refund amount will be before confirming.","risk":"Race conditions between cancellation and courier state transitions are the highest technical risk. Financial risk from double-refunds or incorrect refund tier calculation. Compliance risk if audit trail is incomplete — payment processor disputes require full cancellation records. Reputational risk if couriers deliver cancelled orders.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Senders (Order Creators)","impact":"Primary users of the feature. Need clear UX, transparent refund policy, and confidence that cancellation is final and refund is processed."},{"group":"Couriers","impact":"Must be reliably notified of cancellations to avoid wasted trips. Post-pickup cancellations create return-to-sender work. Courier compensation policy for cancelled-after-assignment orders must be defined."},{"group":"Recipients","impact":"May be expecting a package that is now cancelled. Consider whether recipients should receive a notification that the expected delivery has been cancelled by the sender."},{"group":"Finance / Accounting","impact":"Refund processing, reconciliation, and revenue recognition all depend on accurate cancellation records and correct refund tier application."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle escalations for rejected cancellations, refund disputes, and edge cases. Need access to CancellationEvent audit trail."},{"group":"B2B Partners / API Integrators","impact":"If orders are placed via API, cancellation must be available via API with the same rules. Partner SLAs may have different cancellation terms."}]},"confidence":{"level":45,"reasoning":"The spec is a single sentence — 'Users should be able to cancel their order.' This provides zero detail on refund policy, eligible states, authorization rules, courier handling, or audit requirements. Every business rule above is inferred from standard logistics domain knowledge, not from an actual specification. The refund tiers, cancellation fees, multi-package behavior, and B2B rules are all assumptions that require product confirmation. Multiple critical ambiguities remain unresolved.","high_confidence_areas":["Cancelled is a terminal state (universal domain truth)","Only the order creator should be able to cancel (standard authorization pattern)","Cancellation eligibility depends on order lifecycle state","An audit trail is required for financial operations"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact refund tier percentages and cancellation fee amounts","Whether post-pickup cancellation is allowed at all (some businesses prohibit it)","Multi-package partial cancellation policy","B2B/API cancellation terms","Whether recipients should be notified","Courier compensation policy for cancelled assignments","Promo code / voucher restoration policy"],"assumptions":["The order lifecycle includes states: Pending, Assigned, PickedUp, InTransit, Delivered, Cancelled, Failed","Cancellation is allowed up through PickedUp status — some businesses may cut off at Assigned","Refund tiers exist (full, partial-minus-fee, partial-minus-fee-and-return) — actual amounts are configurable","The system has a courier notification mechanism","Financial audit trail is a hard requirement"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec does not define which order statuses are cancellable. Post-pickup cancellation is operationally expensive and some logistics businesses explicitly prohibit it. This is the single most important product decision for this feature.","affected_stakeholders":["Senders","Couriers","Finance","Operations"],"options":["Allow cancellation only while Pending (before any courier action)","Allow cancellation up to Assigned (before pickup)","Allow cancellation up to PickedUp/InTransit (with return-to-sender)"],"recommendation":"Allow up to Assigned for self-service cancellation. Post-pickup cancellation should require customer support intervention to handle return logistics manually. This balances user flexibility with operational complexity."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No refund policy is specified. The cancellation fee structure, refund percentages, and payment reversal method (void vs. refund) are undefined.","affected_stakeholders":["Senders","Finance","Customer Support"],"options":["100% refund at all cancellable stages","Tiered refund based on fulfillment progress","No refund after courier assignment"],"recommendation":"Tiered refund: 100% if Pending, (100% minus flat cancellation fee) if Assigned. Requires Finance sign-off on fee amount."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"No mention of courier compensation. When a courier is assigned and the order is cancelled, does the courier receive partial compensation for the wasted trip? This affects courier satisfaction and retention.","affected_stakeholders":["Couriers","Finance","Operations"],"options":["No courier compensation for cancelled orders","Flat fee compensation for cancelled-after-assignment","Distance-based compensation"],"recommendation":"Flat fee compensation for couriers who were assigned — critical for courier retention and platform trust."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No mention of whether the recipient should be notified of cancellation. If a recipient is tracking or expecting a delivery, silent cancellation creates a poor experience.","affected_stakeholders":["Recipients","Customer Support"],"options":["No recipient notification","Notify recipient that the delivery has been cancelled by the sender"],"recommendation":"Notify the recipient to reduce inbound support contacts asking 'where is my package?'"}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Order cancellation in a logistics/courier context. A user (shipper/sender) wants to cancel a previously placed delivery order. Cancellation eligibility and consequences depend heavily on where the order sits in its lifecycle — an order that has already been picked up by a courier is fundamentally different from one that is still awaiting assignment.","bounded_context":"Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Order","definition":"A request for cou
